Japanese: ２月２３日　午前　９時３４分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 23, 9:34 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥いかがでしたかしら、 成歩堂弁護士。きのうの調査は。
English: How did the investigation go yesterday, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 正直なところ‥‥まだハッキリ しない点がいくつか、あります。
English: Frankly, there are still a lot of... gray areas.

Japanese: ‥‥というか、 ハッキリしない点だらけだよ‥‥
English: Or rather, the whole thing is one big gray area...

Japanese: どんな判決になっても、 気に病むことはありません。
English: Don't worry about me, no matter what the outcome.

Japanese: カクゴはできていますから。
English: I'm ready to accept my fate.

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、信じてるから。 お姉ちゃんのコト‥‥
English: I believe in you, sis.

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士。センパイとして １つだけ、忠告しておきます。
English: Mr. Wright, let me offer you a word of advice.

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Yes?

Japanese: 弁護士は、依頼人を信じるなど もってのほか。
English: A defense attorney should never "believe" their client.

Japanese: 被告人は、疑われる理由が あるから、起訴される‥‥
English: The defendant is called to trial because they are suspected of wrongdoing!

Japanese: それを忘れないことです。
English: Never forget that.

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさん。
English: Ms. Skye, you...

Japanese: あなたは、千尋さんの先輩で、 あのひとに、よく似ています。
English: You remind me a lot of Mia.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。おふたりには、決定的に ちがう点が、１つあります。
English: But there is one decisive difference between you and her.

Japanese: それは？
English: And that is?

Japanese: あなたは、弁護士ではありません。
English: You're not a defense attorney.

Japanese: そろそろ時間ですね。
English: I believe it's almost time for the trial.

Japanese: それでは、おねがいします。 ‥‥弁護士さん。
English: Good luck, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんがいない、 初めての法廷‥‥か）
English: (My first trial without a Fey helping me...)

Japanese: （それって、つまり‥‥）
English: (No one's going to bail me out this time...)

Japanese: （‥‥だれのチカラも借りない。 ぼく自身が、見つけるんだ）
English: (I'll be alone in there...)

Japanese: （この手で‥‥真実を！）
English: (So I have to discover the truth all by myself!)

Japanese: がんばりましょう、成歩堂さん！ あたしがついてますからね！
English: Let's do it, Mr. Wright! I'll be with you the whole way!

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 23, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: これより、宝月　巴の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Ms. Lana Skye.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側‥‥もとより。
English: The prosecution has been ready for a while, Your Honor.
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （あの事件以来、ひさしぶりだな。 ‥‥法廷に立つのは）
English: (I haven't been in court since Edgeworth's trial... It's been a while now.)

Japanese: ‥‥よけいな感情をまじえる 必要はない、成歩堂　龍一。
English: I hope that personal feelings will not be a part of the proceedings today, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: まわりの者が何を言おうが、 自分の進むべき道は、私が決める。
English: I will choose the path I think is right, regardless of what those around me might say.

Japanese: 外野にはしょせん、 判決を変えることはできないのだ。
English: The judgment to be made here is in our hands, not those of anyone else.

Japanese: それでは、御剣検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well, Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement please.

Japanese: 宝月　巴は、主席検事でありながら ユルされざる罪を犯した。
English: Chief Prosecutor Lana Skye has committed an unpardonable crime.

Japanese: しかも‥‥検事局を犯行現場に 選ぶという暴挙ッ！
English: Not only this, but she was rash enough to commit it in the Prosecutor's Office lot!

Japanese: くうう‥‥やっぱり、ナマは ハクリョクがちがいますね。
English: Wow... He's much more forceful in person.

Japanese: なんかあたし、いろいろ 白状したくなってきちゃいました。
English: I suddenly feel like confessing to everything!

Japanese: しかし‥‥その暴挙が イノチ取りとなったようだ。
English: However, she will now pay for her rashness with her life.

Japanese: 犯行は、ある証人に目撃された。 いわば“プロ”と呼べる証人に！
English: There was a witness to her crime...
A "professional" witness!

Japanese: それでは、その証人を 入廷させていただきましょう！
English: Well then, call your first witness, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 市ノ谷　響華を証人席へ。
English: The prosecution calls its first witness, Ms. Angel Starr, to the stand.

Japanese: （《ゲロまみれのおキョウ》さん、 か‥‥）
English: (The "Cough-up Queen"...?)

Japanese: ええと、あなたはどこかで‥‥
English: Hmm? Haven't I seen you somewhere...?

Japanese: あら、こちら。キャビア弁当で よろしかったかしら。
English: You ordered the Caviar Lunch, right?

Japanese: おほッ！　これが、キャビア！ 生まれて初めて食べますぞッ！
English: Ho ho! Caviar! I've never eaten caviar before!

Japanese: （ナミダ目で メシをかきこんでる‥‥）
English: (The judge is really wolfing it down...)

Japanese: そちらさまも。‥‥ふりかけ弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: Ah, and for you... I have a Fiesta Bowl.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥どうも。
English: Uh... thanks.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と職業を。
English: Will the witness state her name and profession?

Japanese: それで、そちらさまは‥‥ ゴマ弁当でよろしかったかしら？
English: Ah, and you, sir... Did you order "The Fingerprint" lunchbox?

Japanese: ランチタイムには、まだ早い。 名前と、職業をおねがいする。
English: It is too early for lunch. Your name and profession, please.

Japanese: そちらさま、いかがかしら？ お味のほうは。
English: Well, Your Honor? How does it taste?

Japanese: これが、アコガレのキャビア‥‥ 脳がとろけそうですぞッ！
English: So this is why everyone raves about caviar! It's so tasty it hurts!

Japanese: 私、キャビアとは、ヒジキに 似たものだと思ってました！
English: I always thought caviar would taste like pickled tapioca.

Japanese: （どんなイメージだよ‥‥）
English: (What the heck does pickled tapioca taste like!?)

Japanese: ‥‥名前と、職業だ。
English: Name. Profession. Now.

Japanese: 市ノ谷　響華でございますわ。 ‥‥以後、お見知りおきを。
English: Me? The name is Angel Starr. Don't go forgetting it.

Japanese: ワケあって《ベントーランド》にて 陣頭指揮をとっております。
English: I find myself running Lunchland these days.

Japanese: これでよろしかったかしら？ ‥‥御剣検事サマ？
English: Is... that what you wanted me to say, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: それでは、証人。 事件の説明をおねがいしよう。
English: Very well, witness. Please describe the incident to us.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ ‥‥もうヒトクチ食べちゃいます。
English: The prosecution will wait! I'm not finished eating...

Japanese: （早くしろよ！）
English: (Hurry it up!)

Japanese: ふう‥‥さて、御剣検事。
English: Mmmm... Very well, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 事件のセツメイですが、 できれば、警察関係者のカタに‥‥
English: As you know, we usually call on the police to provide a description of the crime....

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長さま。そこの検事さんが おっしゃったでしょう。
English: Your Honor, as Mr. Edgeworth has said to the court...

Japanese: この市ノ谷　響華は、 “プロである”と。
English: I am a... "professional."

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥ どういうことですかな？
English: Uh... Huh? What exactly does that mean?

Japanese: 市ノ谷　響華は、２年前まで 捜査一課の特別捜査官‥‥
English: Until two years ago, Ms. Angel Starr was a special investigator with the police.

Japanese: 甲種殺人事件担当の 刑事だったのだ。
English: She was a first-rate homicide detective.

Japanese: え‥‥ええええッ！ おキョウさんが、刑事ッ！
English: Wh-whaaaat!? Ms. Starr was a detective!?

Japanese: ああああああああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa-hah!

Japanese: あ、あなたは‥‥もしや！ 《ゲロまみれ》の‥‥？
English: I-I know who you are!!! Cough-up...!?

Japanese: おキョウですわ。 ‥‥おひさしゅう、裁判長さま。
English: Cough-up Queen Angel Starr, Your Honor. Long time no see.

Japanese: わ、わわ、わかりました！ せ。せ、説明をおねがいします！
English: V-v-very well! Y-you may continue with the description, Ms. Starr!

Japanese: （‥‥ナニモノなんだ、 あのネエさん‥‥）
English: (Just who is this lady!?)

Japanese: それでは、みなさま。 こちらをごらんくださいな。
English: If I might have the court's attention over here...

Japanese: 検事局の地下駐車場は、２つの ブロックにわかれておりますの。
English: The parking lot at the Prosecutor's Office is divided into two blocks.

Japanese: Ａブロックは検事局職員用、 Ｂブロックは外来者・来客用‥‥
English: A Block is for the Prosecutor's Office personnel.
B Block is for visitors and clients...

Japanese: ２つのブロックは金網で 仕切られておりますわ。
English: A chain divider separates the two blocks.

Japanese: まあ‥‥部外者に侵入されては コマりますからなあ。
English: I suppose that's to keep visitors from taking up prosecutor's spaces, yes.

Japanese: 事件現場は、Ａブロック奥の クルマ‥‥その、後部トランク。
English: The crime took place by a car in the back of A Block, in the car's trunk.

Japanese: 犯人は、被害者をナイフで殺害後、 クルマで運びだそうとしました。
English: The killer stabbed the victim with a knife and went to drive the body out.

Japanese: でも‥‥運悪く、目撃者によって 現行犯タイホされましたの。
English: Unfortunately for her there was a witness, and an arrest was made on the spot.

Japanese: その、勇敢な“目撃者” というのが‥‥？
English: And who was this valiant "witness"...?

Japanese: このおキョウですわ。 ‥‥裁判長さま。
English: Why, it was me, Your Honor.

Japanese: 証拠品《地下駐車場の上面図》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Floor Plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証人。あなたは、 犯行の瞬間を目撃したのですか？
English: Witness, did you see the very moment of the crime?

Japanese: もちろんですわ。
English: Of course, Your Honor.

Japanese: その後ただちに、主席検事サマの 身柄をカクホいたしましたの。
English: Immediately after that, I apprehended the Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥これはもう、 キマリだと思うのですが。
English: Hmm... It seems rather cut and dry, doesn't it?

Japanese: いかがですかな、弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくに同意を 求めないでください。
English: Uh... I can't agree on principle, Your Honor.

Japanese: それでは、裁判長さま。
English: It seems that some poor losers are unwilling to accept the truth, Your Honor.

Japanese: 往生ぎわの悪い殿方を、まっぷたつ にさせていただきますわ。
English: Shall I proceed to crush what little hope they have remaining?

Japanese: たのもしいかぎりです。 ひとつ、おねがいいたしますぞ！
English: If you can... Then give them your worst, Ms. Starr!

Japanese: （まっぷたつ、って‥‥ ぼくのことか？）
English: (Wait, are they talking about me...!?)

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: ワタクシ、わかっておりました。 いつか、こんな日が来ることを‥‥
English: Somehow, I always knew a day like this would come.

Japanese: あの日‥‥カレにお弁当を お持ちする途中のコトでしたのよ。
English: I was on my way to deliver a lunchbox to my boyfriend...

Japanese: 捜査官としてのカンと申すのかしら ‥‥何かを感じてしまいましたの。
English: When I sensed something... perhaps it was my finely-honed detective instincts working.

Japanese: 見ると、金網越しに、シュミの悪い クルマのそばにいる主席検事殿が。
English: Then, through a wire fence, I saw the chief prosecutor standing next to a garish car.

Japanese: 主席検事殿は、右手にナイフを かまえておりましたわね。
English: The chief prosecutor was holding a knife in her right hand....

Japanese: そして‥‥多田敷捜査官の胸に ズブリと突き刺しましたのよ！
English: Then, she thrust the pointy tip of the knife into Detective Goodman's chest!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥コイビトにお弁当。 オトメゴコロ、というヤツですな。
English: Hmm... Bringing a lunchbox to your boyfriend? How touching!

Japanese: フッ‥‥ ギモンの余地はないようだ。
English: Hmph. As you can see... There is no room for doubt.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ナイフをズブリ！　と 刺したところを、そのものズブリ！
English: The key "point" of your testimony seems to be nothing other than...

Japanese: 目撃しているワケですからなあ。
English: the point of the knife which you saw being stabbed into Detective Goodman!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがでしたかしら？ まっぷたつにされた感想は。
English: So... how does it feel to be so utterly crushed?

Japanese: か‥‥考え中です。
English: I... I'm still thinking about that.

Japanese: き‥‥キズは浅いです、 成歩堂さんっ！
English: I-it's merely a flesh wound, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。尋問を おねがいします‥‥さっさと！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: ワタクシ、わかっておりました。 いつか、こんな日が来ることを‥‥
English: Somehow, I always knew a day like this would come.

Japanese: あの日‥‥カレにお弁当を お持ちする途中のコトでしたのよ。
English: I was on my way to deliver a lunchbox to my boyfriend...

Japanese: 捜査官としてのカンと申すのかしら ‥‥何かを感じてしまいましたの。
English: When I sensed something... perhaps it was my finely-honed detective instincts working.

Japanese: 見ると、金網越しに、シュミの悪い クルマのそばにいる主席検事殿が。
English: Then, through a wire fence, I saw the chief prosecutor standing next to a garish car.

Japanese: 主席検事殿は、右手にナイフを かまえておりましたわね。
English: The chief prosecutor was holding a knife in her right hand....

Japanese: そして‥‥多田敷捜査官の胸に、 ズブリと突き刺しましたのよ！
English: Then, she thrust the pointy tip of the knife into Detective Goodman's chest!

Japanese: 殺人は、すべてが計画的だった！ ゴム手袋が、いい証拠じゃないか。
English: The murder was planned! The rubber gloves prove it!

Japanese: き‥‥キズは浅いです、 成歩堂さんっ！
English: I-it's only a flesh wound, Mr. Wright! We can make it!

Japanese: ‥‥さっきも聞いたよ、それ。 他に何かないのかな。
English: You said that before. Anything else?

Japanese: おキョウさんの証言、カガク的に、 まったくスキがありません！
English: Scientifically speaking... Ms. Starr's testimony... is flawless.

Japanese: （致命傷じゃないか！ ‥‥のっけから）
English: (Sounds pretty fatal to me.)

Japanese: どど、どうしましょう！　もう ユーザイなんですか、お姉ちゃん！
English: Wh-what do we do!? Is this it? Is my sister guilty!?

Japanese: とりあえず、アセらず じっくりゆさぶっていこうよ。
English: Let's just keep our heads cool and press the witness a bit, shall we?

Japanese: （となりでアタフタされると、 かえって落ちついちゃうな‥‥）
English: (For some reason, having her panicking next to me makes me calmer...)

Japanese: に、ニコニコしないでくださいっ！
English: D-don't smile like that!

Japanese: なぜ、そんなことを‥‥？
English: How did you know!?

Japanese: 検事サマの、罪をにくむ感情‥‥ ソンケイすべき点もありますわ。
English: I respect the prosecutors' basic abhorrence of crime.

Japanese: しかし。彼らのやり方は、 みにくくユガんでいる。
English: Yet their methods are ugly and twisted.

Japanese: ユガんだやり方は、 いつかヒゲキを生み出すもの。
English: Twisted methods will always lead to tragedy.

Japanese: ‥‥弁当屋の意見として、 ありがたく受けとめておこう。
English: The lunchlady's uninformed opinion is duly noted.

Japanese: ツゴウの悪い証拠品はモミ消して、 存在しなかったことにする‥‥
English: Given that they are used to erasing inconvenient evidence at their whim...

Japanese: そのためなら、知りすぎた捜査官を 切り捨てる‥‥お手のモノだねェ。
English: Killing off a detective that knew too much is merely an extension of that.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥何か、 検事さんにウラミでもあるのかな？
English: Ms. Starr... do you have something personal against prosecutors?

Japanese: 私、捜査官を天職とココロ得て おりましたわ。おそらく‥‥
English: I felt that I had found my dream job when I became an investigator...

Japanese: そこのすました検事さんたちに 解雇通知を受けなければ‥‥今も。
English: And if I hadn't been laid off by those prosecutors over there, I'd still be one.

Japanese: カイコ通知？ （クビにされたのか‥‥）
English: Laid off...? (She was fired...)

Japanese: ワタクシにとって“検事”は、 虫けらにもおよばぬ存在。
English: To me, prosecutors are nothing more than worms.

Japanese: さりとて、ワタクシも“プロ”。 証言にクモリはございませんのよ。
English: That said, I am a pro, as you know. My testimony is unbiased... and flawless.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。それでは、 つづけていただきましょう。
English: Very well. You may continue, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: “カレ”というのは、捜査官の？
English: This boyfriend... he's the detective?

Japanese: そちらのカレではありませんの。 守衛室のカレですわ。
English: Not that boyfriend. The security guard.

Japanese: そ、“そちらのカレ”‥‥ カレをたくさんお持ちなのですか？
English: Th-"that" boyfriend? You have... several?

Japanese: 他にも、“こちらのカレ”や “あちらのカレ”もございますわ。
English: Yes. "This" boyfriend, "that" boyfriend, and "the other" boyfriend.

Japanese: いっそ、裁判長もいかがかしら？ “どちら”があいておりますわよ。
English: Care to join?
The "yet another" boyfriend position is still open for applicants.

Japanese: け、ケッコウです！
English: I-I'll stick with the lunch, thanks.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長さん、 ここで少し考える、と。
English: Note to self: the judge had to think before replying.

Japanese: 守衛室は、駐車場のＡブロックに ございますのよ。
English: The security guard room is in the lot, in A Block.

Japanese: 駐車場をすべて見わたせるように、 ２階部分にございますの。
English: It's up on the second level so you can see everything from there.

Japanese: （あの“ＳＥＣＵＲＩＴＹ”って 書いてある部屋だな‥‥）
English: (That would be the room with the "SECURITY" sign.)

Japanese: ところで、あなた自身はクルマで お弁当を持ってきたのですか‥‥？
English: Incidentally, did you bring your lunchboxes by car?

Japanese: ワタクシ、今は部外者ですから。 Ｂブロックに駐車いたしましたわ。
English: Since I'm a visitor now, I parked in B Block.

Japanese: つまり。おキョウさんは Ｂブロックで事件を目撃した‥‥
English: So... she was in B Block when she witnessed the crime.

Japanese: 事件の起こる“予感”が したわけですか？
English: You "sensed" something? So, you're saying you had a premonition of the murder?

Japanese: あの感じ‥‥なんと申し上げたら よろしいのかしら。
English: It felt like... how would you say...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。実のつまったカボチャを 前にしたときのような、あの感覚。
English: Oh yes...
It was like the feeling you get when you view a pumpkin chock full of seeds!

Japanese: マッタク想像がつきません。
English: I have no idea what that means.

Japanese: “捜査官のカン”と言えば‥‥
English: Speaking of a "detective's instincts"...

Japanese: 被害者の多田敷　道夫さんも 捜査官でしたね？
English: Wasn't the victim, Mr. Bruce Goodman, also a detective?

Japanese: ウデはまだまだでしたけれど。 浅づけのタクアン、でしたわね。
English: Yes, well, he was like a young cheese.

Japanese: タクアン‥‥
English: A... young cheese?

Japanese: まだ、色に染まっていない‥‥ まっすぐで、スナオな子だった。
English: A pale white cheese, not yet tangy with experience on the streets. A greenhorn.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥私などはもう、 まっ黄色でシワシワですがね。
English: Hmm... I, of course, am hard, yellowed, sharp as a tack.

Japanese: ヘンなニオイもありそうですよね。
English: I bet you stink, too.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、あのとき。 ワタクシ、何か胸さわぎがして‥‥
English: In any case, there, in the lot, I felt something stirring in the back of my mind...

Japanese: シュミの悪いクルマというのは、 モチロン‥‥
English: By "garish car," you mean...

Japanese: 御剣検事サマのクルマですわ。
English: Mr. Edgeworth's car, yes.

Japanese: み、御剣検事の‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth's!?

Japanese: ちなみに。死体に刺さっていた ナイフも、検事サマのモノ‥‥
English: Incidentally, the knife with which the victim was stabbed was also Mr. Edgeworth's.

Japanese: そうでしたわね？
English: Wasn't it?

Japanese: まちがいない。
English: Indeed, it was.

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふむう‥‥マッタク、 ビミョーな事件です。
English: Hmm! What an odd case this is.

Japanese: それで、あなたの見た人物は‥‥ 被告人にマチガイないのですかな？
English: And the person you saw... you are sure it was the defendant?

Japanese: ワタクシ、現場から１０メートルも 離れていない場所で見ましたのよ。
English: I saw her from no further than thirty feet away.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがえようが ないだろうねえ‥‥
English: I am certain it was her.

Japanese: （もし、これが本当なら‥‥ 攻めようがないぞ！）
English: (If she's telling the truth, we're doomed!)

Japanese: とりあえず、つけてみましょうよ！ ケチの１つでも。
English: Let's just do what we can! Even if we don't have any proof, we can always complain!

Japanese: ‥‥相手が御剣だからなあ。 何か材料があればいいんだけど‥‥
English: We're dealing with Edgeworth here... I need better material before I start pressing this.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それで、あなたの見た 被告人は、どんなようすでしたか？
English: Hmm... Can you tell us what the suspect was doing when you saw her?

Japanese: 証人！　あなたは先ほどの証言で ハッキリ、こう言っています！
English: Witness! In your testimony, you clearly stated the following:

Japanese: “検事は虫けらにもおよばぬ存在” ‥‥それならば！
English: Prosecutors are nothing more than worms. Ergo!

Japanese: あなたは、ある“悪意”を持って 犯行を目撃していたことに‥‥
English: You are a biased witness!

Japanese: ‥‥クチを つつしむんだねえ、ボウヤ‥‥
English: You might want to keep those silly opinions to YOURSELF in the future, rookie.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh? Rookie?

Japanese: おキョウのコトバにアヤをつける ‥‥カクゴはいいんだろうねぇ？
English: Unless you're willing to risk the consequences of doubting me?

Japanese: 初めちょろちょろ、なかパッパ。 おいしく炊きあげられたいかいッ！
English: I'll fry you like a fritter! Crispy on the outside... chewy on the inside!!!

Japanese: で‥‥出ました！ おキョウさんのグルメタンカ！
English: That... That was inspiring!

Japanese: ‥‥“新米弁護士”と“新米”を かけたものと思われる、と。
English: I believe I've heard that tag line elsewhere... you could cry plagiarism?

Japanese: 弁当屋に身をやつしていても スジはとおす。ワタクシなりにッ！
English: I may be relegated to the lowly post of lunchlady... But my instincts are honed!

Japanese: こ‥‥‥この写真はッ！ あなたが撮影したのですか！
English: A-a photograph! You took this!?

Japanese: 事件に気づいた、その瞬間。 反射的にシャッターをひとつ。
English: The moment I witnessed the crime, my reflexes took over and snap! I took a picture.

Japanese: ワタクシ‥‥弁当の１つに カメラを仕込んでありますのよ。
English: In fact... One of my lunchboxes is rigged with a camera.

Japanese: （ふつうにクビから ぶら下げておけばいいのに）
English: (I suppose that's more exciting than just hanging it around your neck.)

Japanese: このような写真‥‥ 私も初めて見るが？
English: Er... this is my first time seeing this photograph.

Japanese: 検事サマに見せたら、何をされるか わかりませんからねえ。
English: You think I'd show it to you, a prosecutor? Think again.

Japanese: ‥‥ワタクシ、捜査課の写真班に カレがいますのよ。
English: My boyfriend works in the photography division of Criminal Affairs.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥これは被告人に まちがいないようです！
English: Well... This is most certainly the defendant!

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （マズいな‥‥これはたしかに、 ともえさんにマチガイないぞ！）
English: (Uh oh, that is unmistakably Lana Skye!)

Japanese: それで‥‥このとき、 被告人はいったい‥‥？
English: So, what was the defendant doing at the time?

Japanese: ハンニンが手にしていた“ナイフ” について、聞かせてください。
English: Tell me more about this knife that the suspect was carrying.

Japanese: そう‥‥刃わたり１０センチほど、 でしたかしら。
English: Well, I'd say the blade was about 10 centimeters long.

Japanese: いかがかしら？　御剣検事サマ。 ‥‥あなたのナイフでしょう？
English: Is that right, Mr. Edgeworth? It is your knife, after all.

Japanese: む‥‥むう。 まあ、そんなところだろう。
English: Er... Ahem, yes, that's about right.

Japanese: ‥‥検事サマたちは、 人体の急所にはお詳しいはず。
English: Prosecutors are, by nature, well-versed in the location of a man's vital organs.

Japanese: ゆでたまごを作るよりヤサシイ 仕事だったでしょうねえ‥‥
English: I'm sure it was easier than boiling an egg for my egg salad surprise set.

Japanese: そ。それは、あなたに 証言できることではありません！
English: Y-you can't testify as to her ability to kill an egg! I mean, a person!

Japanese: あらあら‥‥そちら、 なまたまご派でしたのかしら？
English: Hmm? Perhaps a chicken salad set would have been a better metaphor?

Japanese: ‥‥それで。ナイフを かまえた被告人は、どうしたか？
English: So, the defendant was holding a knife. What then?

Japanese: あなたはなぜ、犯行を 止めようとしなかったのですか？
English: Tell the court why you didn't try to stop this crime!

Japanese: ナイフを振り上げたところを 見ていたんですよね？
English: You did see her raise the knife to strike, no?

Japanese: むぅ‥‥ たしかに、そうですな。
English: Hmm... The defense has a point.

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥気づいたとき には、すでに遅すぎましたの。
English: Unfortunately, by the time I realized what was going on, it was already too late.

Japanese: 遅すぎた‥‥？
English: Too late...?

Japanese: 次の瞬間‥‥主席検事殿は、凶器を 振り下ろしておりましたわ！
English: Yes... the next moment... The chief prosecutor brought down the murder weapon!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですか‥‥
English: I... I see.

Japanese: でも！　もしかしたら、 オシャレ手袋だったのかも‥‥
English: What if she was just in the habit of wearing gloves? Like, driving gloves?

Japanese: 被告人がタイホされたとき、 手袋はカクニンされている！
English: The gloves were admitted as evidence when the defendant was arrested!

Japanese: 解剖のときに使用される、 ゴム製の特殊なものだった。
English: They were rubber gloves, of the kind used for autopsies!

Japanese: つまり‥‥主席検事サマは、 被害者と現場で会ったとき‥‥
English: In other words, when the chief prosecutor came to the crime scene...

Japanese: 最初から殺害を考えていた。 ‥‥そう考えるべきですわね。
English: She came to do murder! It's the only possible conclusion one can make.

Japanese: 犯行のジュンビは、すべて ととのっていた、ってコトさね！
English: Everything was planned, it was a premeditated crime!

Japanese: ぐぐぐうううっ！
English: Arrrrgh!

Japanese: た、たしかに！ さすが、元・捜査官ですなあ。
English: Impressive! I'm sorry they took you off the force, Ms. Starr!

Japanese: （マズいな‥‥）
English: (This is bad...)

Japanese: （この殺人は “すべてが”計画的だった‥‥）
English: (She's got them thinking this was all planned...)

Japanese: （この主張がとおってしまえば、 いきなりオシマイだぞ‥‥！）
English: (If she can prove this claim, the trial's already over!)

Japanese: （“計画的ではなかった”部分‥‥ そいつを探してみるか！）
English: (I've got to think of a way to show that this wasn't premeditated!)

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、 それを目撃したわけですか？
English: And you witnessed this?

Japanese: ともえさんが、ナイフで被害者を “刺す”‥‥その瞬間を。
English: You saw Ms. Skye stab the victim with the knife?

Japanese: すでに申し上げたとおり。 ‥‥この、ソース弁当にかけて！
English: As I've already said, yes! I swear it on my finest "Salmon Swirl" lunch!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥！ 食欲をそそるニオイですなあ。
English: Hmm!!! I'm sure that is a fine lunch!

Japanese: しかし‥‥それはオカシイのです。 この写真を見てください！
English: But... isn't that odd? Look at this photograph!

Japanese: あなたの撮影した、この写真‥‥
English: This is the photograph you took of the very moment of the crime, is it not?

Japanese: 巴さんは、ナイフなど 持っていないッ！
English: Then why is Ms. Skye not holding a knife!?
... Ahem.

Japanese: 御剣検事。 何かコメントをおねがいします。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, your thoughts?

Japanese: 異議あり。
English: Objection.

Japanese: ‥‥もう少し ヤル気を出して行きましょうよ。
English: Let's be a little more careful with our evidence, shall we?

Japanese: ヤル気を出すべきは キサマのほうだ、弁護士。
English: It is you that needs to be more careful, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: この写真は‥‥犯行直前に 撮影されたものではない。
English: This photograph was not taken the moment before the stabbing...

Japanese: シャッターが切られたのは すでにナイフを刺した“あと”だ。
English: This was taken the moment AFTER the stabbing!

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてそんなコトが わかるんですかッ！
English: H-how can you tell that!?

Japanese: ‥‥返り血。
English: Blood splatter.
Huh?

Japanese: 主席検事サマのコートの 赤いシミ‥‥見えませんかしら？
English: See the dark crimson stain on the Chief Prosecutor's coat?

Japanese: （シロクロ写真じゃないか！）
English: (It's a black-and-white photograph!)

Japanese: はああ‥‥わかりにくいですが、 これは血痕だったのですか‥‥
English: Ah... yes, it's hard to tell, but this could be blood.

Japanese: いかがかな、弁護人。 問題はマッタク、見あたらないが。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? I see no problem here.

Japanese: ‥‥“キミ”以外は。
English: No problem... except you.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　モンダイ児あつかい されてますよ！
English: Mr. Wright! Are you going to just sit there and take that kind of abuse!?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥どうなんだろう‥‥）
English: (Argh... You got a better idea?)

Japanese: 問題ありませんね。 たしかに‥‥ぼく以外は。
English: Now that you mention it, I see no problem here.
Other than myself.

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん！　認めちゃダメ！ せつないですっ！
English: M-Mr. Wright! You can't just let him walk all over you! Th-that's just sad!

Japanese: せつないアナタには‥‥ 初恋弁当。いかがだったかしら？
English: Feeling sad, Mr. Wright? Perhaps a special "Lover's Lunchbox" would cheer you up?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら 時間のムダだったようです。
English: Well, that was a waste of time.

Japanese: それでは、証言に 戻っていただきましょう。
English: Let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: （もっと追求したほうが よかったかな‥‥）
English: (Perhaps I should have dug a little deeper...)

Japanese: しかし！　さっき証人は ハッキリ証言した！
English: Wait! That contradicts what the witness said in her testimony!

Japanese: 『事件に気づいた“瞬間”に シャッターを押した』と！
English: Namely, that she took the picture the "moment" she witnessed the crime!

Japanese: コトバが足りませんでしたわね。 ‥‥ごめんあそばせ。
English: Well, it seems I was slightly unclear. My apologies.

Japanese: そ、それで終わりですか。
English: Th-that's it!?

Japanese: ‥‥ゴハンが足りなければ、 おかわりをすれば済むコト。
English: If you run out of lunch, you order seconds. Problem solved!

Japanese: それがイヤなら、最初から 大盛りを注文するコトだねッ！
English: If you don't like it, try ordering the jumbo sized lunch from the get-go!

Japanese: ワケもわからず 怒られちゃいましたね。
English: Good advice. I'm not sure I understood it, but... good advice.

Japanese: ワタクシには、彼女の犯行を止める ヨユウはありませんでしたの。
English: I didn't have time to stop her.

Japanese: 宝月検事サマは、プログラムされた ロボットのように、セイカクに‥‥
English: Prosecutor Skye was cold, calculating, like a robot.

Japanese: そして冷酷に、殺人計画を 実行したのですわ！
English: She killed without pain or remorse! It was a premeditated murder!

Japanese: け、“計画”‥‥ なぜ、わかるんですか！
English: P-premeditated!? How do you know!?

Japanese: ‥‥写真の、主席検事の手に 注目していただきたい。
English: Look at the chief prosecutor's hands in that photograph.

Japanese: これは‥‥ “手袋”ですかな‥‥？
English: Well...! Are those... gloves?

Japanese: うすい、ゴム製の手袋だろう。 彼女はなぜ、こんな特殊なものを？
English: Surgical gloves made of thin rubber, most likely. Why would she have those on?
Uh...

Japanese: 犯行計画なくして、 こんな手袋をしているハズがない！
English: If it was not premeditated, she would not be wearing those gloves!

Japanese: ぐはあああああッ！
English: Waaaaaargh!

Japanese: たしかに、この手袋は 犯行の計画性を物語っています！
English: These gloves do seem to tell a tale of premeditation!

Japanese: “計画殺人”‥‥重罪ですわね。
English: Premeditated murder... a serious offense.

Japanese: ‥‥証人！ 証言に追加していただきましょう！
English: Witness! Add this to your testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。これがなんだか、 おわかりですか？
English: Witness, do you know what this is?

Japanese: このおキョウを試そうなんて １０年早くてよ。
English: Are you trying to test me? I sell box lunches for a living, you know.

Japanese: モチロン、凶器ですわ。‥‥そこの 検事サマのトランクに入っていた！
English: That's a knife. THE knife.
The knife that was in Mr. Edgeworth's trunk!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。私のナイフだ。
English: Indeed, it is my knife.

Japanese: どうなってるんだよ、この事件‥‥
English: What's with this case!?

Japanese: 赤い凶器も、赤いクルマも‥‥ あの赤い検事さんのですって！
English: The bloody murder weapon, a red car... all belonging to the prosecutor there!?

Japanese: ‥‥そもそも、被告人が 地方検事局の主席検事、だろう？
English: The defendant is the chief prosecutor for the district, right?

Japanese: ママー。どうなってるの？ 検事局のおじちゃんたち‥‥
English: Mommy, are prosecutors bad people?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側から、要請します。
English: The defense has a request.

Japanese: 証人は、セイカクな証言を してください！
English: We ask that the witness provide an ACCURATE testimony.

Japanese: どういうことかしら？ ボウヤ‥‥
English: What's that, Rookie?

Japanese: あなたは、こう証言しました。
English: In your testimony...

Japanese: 『宝月　巴は、あらかじめ犯行を “すべて”計画していた』
English: You stated that Lana Skye planned this murder.

Japanese: 『だから、こんな特殊な ゴム手袋を用意していたのだ』
English: And that's why she was wearing those special gloves.

Japanese: 自然なスイリではないですか。 手袋には、計画性を感じますが‥‥
English: Seems like a natural conclusion to me! The gloves do indicate planning...

Japanese: しかし、それならば！
English: However!

Japanese: なぜ、かんじんの“凶器”を 用意していなかったのでしょうか？
English: Why would she not also prepare the most important thing... the murder weapon!?
Oh.

Japanese: このナイフは、たまたまクルマの トランクに入っていたものです。
English: This knife just happened to be in the trunk of that car.

Japanese: もし、殺人を計画するのならば！ まず用意するのが“凶器”です！
English: Ladies and gentlemen, if you're going to plan a murder, you don't forget the weapon!

Japanese: く‥‥‥‥くううううう‥‥ッ！
English: Ugh...Woooooooorrrgh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: （‥‥よし！ これで、状況は変わった！）
English: (Great! Now the tide is turning in our favor!)

Japanese: さすがですっ、成歩堂さん！ お姉ちゃん、これでムザイに‥‥
English: Great show, Mr. Wright! My sister's as good as free!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士クン。 シオ弁当‥‥でよかったかな？
English: Wright.
I believe the next lunch you'll be eating is... humble pie!

Japanese: な、なんだって？
English: W-what!?

Japanese: ‥‥まさか、 こんなササイなことで‥‥
English: I hope you weren't deluding yourself into thinking that the "tide has turned."

Japanese: “状況は変わった”などと カンちがいしていないだろうな。
English: Not over such a trifling detail!

Japanese: し、しかし！　ジッサイ、 殺人の計画性は、これで‥‥
English: B-but this shoots a hole in the whole premeditated theory!

Japanese: ‥‥バカな。
English: Bah!

Japanese: 検察側は、“計画性”など 最初から、モンダイにしていない。
English: The prosecution could care less if it was premeditated or not.

Japanese: ‥‥そこの弁当屋が 勝手に言いだしたことだ。
English: The only one who seems to care is that lunchlady over there.

Japanese: 《被告人・宝月　巴が、 捜査官をナイフで刺殺した》
English: The defendant, Lana Skye, murdered a detective with a knife.

Japanese: 検察側が立証すべきは その１点のみ。‥‥他にはない。
English: That is the only thing the prosecution need prove. Nothing else.

Japanese: フン‥‥！　検事局のボウヤが アタマのいいトコを見せたワケ？
English: Very good, Mr. Prosecutor... I suppose you think you're clever now?

Japanese: でもね。あの女はアキラカに やっつけるつもりだったのさ！
English: But you know as well as I do that she planned on killing him! It was planned!

Japanese: それでなければ、あんな‥‥
English: If she wasn't, why would she have been wearing...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥その先は、証言して いただいたほうがいいようです。
English: I believe I'd like to hear your testimony again.

Japanese: 証人。“見たことだけ”‥‥ おねがいしよう。
English: Witness, please tell us only what you "saw," not what you "thought."

Japanese: おだまりなさいな。
English: How dare you!

Japanese: おキョウのスイリは、ボウヤの目玉 ５０コぶんのビタミンがあるのさ！
English: My powers of deduction are not to be underestimated!

Japanese: （コワいなあ‥‥）
English: (Really now...)

Japanese: ～おキョウのスイリ～
English: -- Angel's Deduction --

Japanese: 宝月　巴は、多田敷捜査官を 殺害するつもりだったのさ！
English: Lana Skye intended to murder Detective Goodman!

Japanese: そのために、わざわざ被害者を 検事局に呼び出したんだからねぇ。
English: That's why she called the victim all the way to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 主席検事サマは、被害者に かなりのウラミがあったハズ。
English: I'm sure the Chief Prosecutor had a grudge against the victim.

Japanese: そうでなきゃ、あの人間マシーンが 何度もナイフを使うはずがない！
English: Nothing else could drive that human machine to plunge the knife in again and again...

Japanese: ‥‥警察局にいたはずの被害者が、 検事局に呼び出された‥‥
English: The victim was summoned from the Police Department to the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: たしかに、そこにはある種の “計画性”が感じられます！
English: It does sound a lot like premeditation, doesn't it!?

Japanese: そんなコト言ってたら、ラーメンの 出前もたのめませんよねー。
English: So, if I order pizza, does that mean I'm planning to kill the delivery boy?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします！
English: In any case, the defense may now cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～おキョウのスイリ～
English: -- Angel's Deduction --

Japanese: 宝月　巴は、多田敷捜査官を 殺害するつもりだったのさ！
English: Lana Skye intended to murder Detective Goodman!

Japanese: そのために、わざわざ被害者を 検事局に呼び出したんだからねぇ。
English: That's why she called the victim all the way to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 主席検事サマは、被害者に かなりのウラミがあったハズ。
English: I'm sure the Chief Prosecutor had a grudge against the victim.

Japanese: そうでなきゃ、あの人間マシーンが 何度もナイフを使うはずがない！
English: Nothing else could drive that human machine to plunge the knife in again and again...

Japanese: （おキョウさん‥‥見た目どおり 短気だったみたいだな）
English: (Ms. Starr has turned out to be as short-tempered as she looked when we met her.)

Japanese: （捜査官のプライドがキズついて、 スキだらけだ‥‥！）
English: (Challenging her abilities as a detective really set her off!)

Japanese: “短気はソン気”‥‥ カガク的にも証明されています！
English: The short wick burns out the fastest! It's a scientific fact!

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、 前から思ってたんだけど‥‥
English: I wonder... wouldn't it depend on the size of the candle?

Japanese: “短気”はわかるんだけど。 なんなんだろ。“ソン気”って。
English: I mean, add more wax and even a really short wick will burn longer...

Japanese: 世の中には、カガクでは 解明できないコトもあります！
English: Obviously, more scientific testing is required!

Japanese: 彼女の赤いマフラーが、血に見えた ‥‥それほど鬼気迫ってましたわ。
English: Her red muffler looked like blood to me... that's how ghastly the whole scene was.

Japanese: だから、どうして それがわかるんですか！
English: You've said that, but you haven't told us how you know!

Japanese: だから、それをこれから 話すんじゃないか！
English: That's what I'm about to tell you, Rookie!

Japanese: 今のは、話の“マクラ”と 言うヤツでしょう。
English: I believe what she just said was a mere prelude to the story she is about to tell.

Japanese: ダマって聞いておくように。
English: Try not to interrupt her again.

Japanese: ボウヤ‥‥いっそ、おキョウの ひざマクラで眠らせてやろうかッ！
English: Rookie...
Never interrupt a storyteller! It's like pulling a bun out of the oven half-baked!

Japanese: （‥‥タンカになってないような）
English: (Something's half-baked here alright, and it's you!)

Japanese: その弁護人なら、自分の ひじマクラでじゅうぶんだろう。
English: Try not to confuse the defense, witness. They're not very quick on their feet.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、被告人に殺意があると 考えたのか？　理由をうかがおう。
English: Now, why did you believe the suspect had intentions to murder the victim?

Japanese: 被告人の行動を見ればわかるさ。
English: Her actions speak for themselves!

Japanese: ともえさんが呼び出したという 証拠はありません！
English: You have no proof that Ms. Skye called him there!

Japanese: 呼び出していないという 証拠もございませんのよ。
English: You have no proof that she didn't!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥御剣検事。 いかがですか？
English: Hmm... Mr. Edgeworth, thoughts?

Japanese: 被告人‥‥宝月主席検事の 携帯電話には、通話の記録はない。
English: There is no record of a call made on the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye's phone.

Japanese: 文通をしていたのかも しれなくてよ。
English: She might have written him a letter!

Japanese: （せめて“公衆電話”ぐらい 言ってほしいな‥‥）
English: (C'mon! You could have tried "public phone" first, at least!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥事件当日。被害者は 検事局を訪れ、殺害された。
English: In any case, the victim came to the Prosecutor's Office, where he was murdered.

Japanese: 何か“理由”があった はず、でしょうなあ。
English: I'm sure he had a reason to be there.

Japanese: 証人。被害者を呼びだしたのが、 なぜ被告人だと思うのですか？
English: Witness?
Why do you think it was the suspect who summoned the victim that day?

Japanese: どんな“ウラミ”だと 言うんですか！
English: What kind of "grudge"!?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 そこまでは知りませんわ。
English: Well, I wouldn't know that.

Japanese: “さあ”じゃありませんよ！
English: Of course you don't! That's because she didn't have a grudge!

Japanese: では‥‥ボウヤ。
English: Rookie...

Japanese: ここにお弁当がございます。 ‥‥オカズは何かしら？
English: I have a lunchbox here. Now... what's inside?

Japanese: わ、わかるワケ、ないでしょう！
English: H-how am I supposed to know!?

Japanese: そう、お弁当はたしかに存在する。 ‥‥でも、中身まではわからない。
English: See? We agree there is a lunchbox here, but we don't know what's inside!

Japanese: 人生は、シャケ弁当のようなもの。 ‥‥そうは思いませんコト？
English: A person's life is like a lunchbox with pretzels. Don't you agree?

Japanese: な、なるほど！　だからシャケは こんなにも、しょっぱいのですね！
English: I-I get it! That's why my lunch was so salty!

Japanese: （たとえ話にヨワいんだよなあ、 あの裁判長‥‥）
English: (This judge isn't very good with metaphors...)

Japanese: 被告人が、多田敷捜査官を ウラんでいた‥‥
English: The suspect had a grudge against Detective Goodman.

Japanese: そう考えた“コンキョ”を 聞かせていただこうか。
English: Will you tell us your basis for thinking this?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなコト‥‥
English: It's simple...

Japanese: “人間”で“マシーン”‥‥ 証人の発言はムジュンしているッ！
English: A "human machine"!? That's a contradiction!

Japanese: ふう‥‥
English: Please...

Japanese: もう少し、マシなケチを おねがいできないだろうか。
English: Can't you find fault with something of substance, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事のタメイキは シトラスの香り、と。
English: Note to self: Mr. Edgeworth's sighs smell like citrus fruit.

Japanese: ええと‥‥“何度も”というのは、 具体的に何回ですか？
English: Umm...
You say "again and again"... how many times did she stab him, exactly?

Japanese: ‥‥キャベツの千切りといって、 １０００回切るワケじゃない‥‥
English: We often say "chop into a thousand pieces," but we don't actually mean 1,000 pieces.

Japanese: セイカクな回数になんの イミがあるのさッ！
English: What difference does it make if the deed is done!?

Japanese: （なんでこっちが 怒られるんだよ‥‥）
English: (How come she's getting mad at me!?)

Japanese: ま。とりあえず、何回か刺した、 というコトにしておきませんか。
English: Let's just say she stabbed him several times and leave it at that.

Japanese: （なんでそんなテキトーな ケツロンに落ちつくんだよ‥‥）
English: (Leave it at that!? This is a murder case, people!)

Japanese: 思ったコトは、クチに出して 行きましょう、成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright, you should speak up if you have an objection, you know!

Japanese: 赤いマフラー‥‥？
English: Her red muffler?

Japanese: 主席検事サマ、いつもクビに お巻きになっておいででしょう？
English: Yes, like a scarf. The Chief Prosecutor always wears one around her neck.

Japanese: ‥‥いつ、吊るし上げられても いいように、ね。
English: So she can be easily hanged at a moment's notice, I suppose...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (She's right...)

Japanese: （ともえさん、赤いマフラーを 巻いていたっけな‥‥）
English: (Ms. Skye was wearing a red scarf... wasn't she?)

Japanese: でも‥‥“返り血”と見まちがえた なんて、不自然な気がしますが。
English: But wait...
Isn't it odd that you mistook that for splattered blood?

Japanese: まあ‥‥私もよく、このヒゲを 見まちがえられますからな。
English: Well, people often mistake my beard...

Japanese: ヨダレかけと。
English: For a bib.

Japanese: （そりゃ、モンダイだろ）
English: (A judge with a bib. That's why this place feels so much like kindergarten sometimes.)

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥
English: Actually...

Japanese: ワタクシ、ムネのあたりに血痕を 見たような気がいたしますの‥‥
English: I do think I saw some traces of blood on her chest...

Japanese: しかし‥‥解剖記録はゼッタイだ。 ナイフのキズは、１カ所のみ。
English: However, the autopsy report is clear on this matter.
There was only one knife wound.

Japanese: （おキョウさん‥‥自分の証言に 自信がないみたいだな）
English: (Apparently, Ms. Starr isn't entirely sure of her own testimony.)

Japanese: ちゃ‥‥ チャンスですぞ、成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright! This is our chance!!!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだよ、それ。
English: Chance for what, I wonder...?

Japanese: “何度も”刺した‥‥ そんなの、目撃できるはずがない！
English: You say she stabbed him again and again...
But you couldn't have witnessed that!

Japanese: このおキョウを試すつもりなら‥‥ １パイ食らわせるよ！
English: Are you testing me...? Then I'll test you!

Japanese: この、青ノリ弁当をッ！
English: With my Moss Surprise!!!

Japanese: ‥‥１パイ食らうのは、 恐らくキミのほうだろう。
English: I'm afraid the moss is growing under our feet as we wait, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: W-what do you mean?

Japanese: 説明するのもバカらしいが‥‥ これを見ていただこう。
English: I shouldn't have to explain this... But, take a look.

Japanese: 《ナイフによるキズは１カ所》‥‥ 解剖記録に、そう書かれている。
English: The autopsy report states that death was due to a loss of blood... from one stab wound.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああッ、ホントです！ さすが、御剣検事！
English: Ah hah! You're right! Good show, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ホレちゃいますよねー！ カッコイイなあ、御剣検事さん。
English: What a hunk! He's my hero, really.

Japanese: （ぼくのチカラいっぱいの “異議あり”はどこへ消えたんだ）
English: (What about my objection? No one noticed?)

Japanese: さあ‥‥いかがですかな、証人！
English: Well, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥こちら。ケチャップ弁当で よろしかったかしら？
English: You got the Crime Scene Set, right?

Japanese: あ、ど。どうも‥‥
English: Uh... oh, thanks.

Japanese: 血とケチャップを見まちがう人間は いない‥‥そう信じておりました。
English: I always believed that no one could ever mistake ketchup for blood....

Japanese: でも、今日からは あらためるコトにいたしますわ。
English: But now, I realize that such mistakes are possible.

Japanese: つまり‥‥ “見まちがい”‥‥だった、と？
English: So... You're saying you mistook something... for blood?

Japanese: ナイフを振り上げたとき‥‥ムネの あたりに見えた気がしましたの。
English: When she lifted her knife, I thought I saw blood at her breast...

Japanese: ‥‥返り血が。
English: Splattered blood from her victim!

Japanese: だから、“少なくとも２回は 刺したはず”と思ってしまった‥‥
English: That's why I thought she must have stabbed him at least twice.

Japanese: “返り血”を何と見まちがえたか ‥‥証言をおねがいします！
English: Then tell us what you saw that you thought was blood! Testify!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
